entry.title: Maximize Value with Value Analytics for SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Anyone looking out for some analytics on SAP Ariba solutions?

Starting with SAP Analytics Cloud Content Release Q2 2024 you will find a new package in your SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere semantic onboarding section by the name of Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement (SAP Datasphere).

The content included serves as a broad foundation to analyze all steps across your sourcing and procurement processes done in the SAP Ariba solution suite. It comes with quite a number of data models in SAP Datasphere that will allow you to get most of your questions answered like

  • how is your company's spend behavior?
  • how can the sourcing / procurement processes be optimized?
  • how can you leverage savings potentials?

You can take this as a starter to get going. And as always you are free to enhance these data models bringing your own data and models for extension cases to get the big picture and reflect data of your company's end-2-end solution landscape.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Stories

The package comes with three SAP Analytics Cloud stories to give you an idea of what can be built on top of the delivered SAP Datasphere data models:

1. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Strategic Spend Overview

This story provides you with insights into the value your sourcing and procurement program delivers. It does so via providing both direct cost savings as well as corresponding KPIs. In addition, the story provides detailed data that allows you to identify root causes for process gaps, allowing you to drive continuous improvement.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Value Analytics customer gain visibility into the ROI they've achieved using Ariba
  • Cost Savings shows spend that has been strategically sourced, price reduction from sourcing events and guided buying RFQs, savings rates as a % of sourced spend and trued-up savings.
  • Spend Compliance tracks year-over-year improvement to compliant spend put through proper buy channels
  • Working Capital measure captured and lost discounts across supply base
  • Process Improvement measure cycle times for all critical source to pay subprocesses like supplier onboarding, sourcing project, contract execution, REQ to PO and invoice processing

Savings/Benefits Analysis Summary

Strategic Spend Overview - Process Improvement

2. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Procurement Operational Spend Overview

This story uncovers the untapped potential of your procurement practice, gaining valuable insights through a holistic operational view of your source to pay process. It will help you to maximize your end-to-end procurement efficiency, mitigate supplier risks and optimize category spend.

The story consists of 5 pages, covering 

  • Supplier Management: Onboarding of suppliers, risk exposure, geographical distribution, registration and qualification status
  • Sourcing: Awarded amounts per event/commodity and risk attached, event leading and pending savings, event participation over time and events per event owner
  • Contracts: Contract amounts per project owner/commodity and attached supplier risks, cycle times per project, distribution of contract types and contract expiration term types, overview of expiring contracts
  • Buying: spend details per cost center/commodity over time, contract vs. non-contract spend, catalog vs. non-catalog spend, approval cycle times , rejections rates and approval time per approver
  • Invoicing: Inovoice amounts per cost center/commodity/over time and cash flow related, amounts per due date

Operational Spend Overview - Supplier Management

Operational Spend Overview - Contracts


3. SAP Ariba Source to Pay - Supplier 360

This story allows you to gain a complete 360 view over your suppliers. ​
Discover a panoramic perspective of your suppliers related procurement spend, from a risk, a geography or even an individual supplier angle. This will help you to identify top-performing suppliers, mitigate supplier risks and increase your suppliers’ overall process compliance.

Supplier 360 Story

 

SAP Datasphere Models

Once imported you can find all related objects in the SAP_CONTENT space. 

The stories in SAP Analytics Cloud and their data models in SAP Datasphere combine data from various Ariba Solutions like:

Supplier Management

  • SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance
  • SAP Ariba Supplier Risk

Sourcing and Contracts

  • SAP Ariba Sourcing
  • SAP Ariba Contracts

Procurement

  • SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • SAP Ariba Guided Buying (Tactical Sourcing)

Please check the image below to get a first understanding of what is included. 

Overview SAP Datasphere Models

The objects have roughly been separated to reflect applications in the SAP Ariba suite, using the following naming convention:

SM -> Supplier Management

SC -> Sourcing & Contracts

BI -> Buying & Invoicing 

Here is one example for analyzing your Invoice line items (based on Analytical API data). It will help you to sort your invoices according to self defined aging buckets and to identify invoices with exceptions easily. 

Data modeling has followed the layered approach as for general content development in SAP Datasphere:

In the Inbound Layer (IL) you will find the local table SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items (LT) (SAP_ARIBA_SRC_INVOICELINEITEMS_AN) and an abstracting view on top SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (IL) (SAP_PROC_BI_IL_InvoiceLineItems) that adds realm specifica like the reporting currency.

In the Harmonization Layer (HL) on top, you will find the Fact View that combines the Invoice Line Item data with the invoice exceptions plus the Date Grid view that "attaches" the dynamic date buckets to your invoice data to let you check on due dates for your invoices.

In the Reporting Layer (RL) on top, the Analytic Model SAP Ariba: Invoice Line Items - A (RL) (SAP_PROC_BI_RL_InvoiceLineItems) defines additional calculated measures and exposes the data to your SAP Analytics Cloud story.

Invoice Line Items with due date aging information

Aging Invoices Per Supplier

In case you are operating on multiple realms, don't shy away, this has been catered for throughout the data models. So you can report on single or multiple realms.

 

Data Integration

How to get your SAP Ariba data into SAP Datasphere?

The data is sourced from SAP Ariba using the following APIs:

  • Analytical API
  • Operational Reporting API for Procurement
  • Operational Reporting API for Strategic Sourcing
  • Supplier Data API
  • Risk Exposure API
  • Master Data Retrieval API for Procurement

For replicating the data from your SAP Ariba applications the Ariba Extractor app is used. The Ariba Extractor is a BTP application  that you can deploy on your BTP account. It can be modified and extended by customers and partners as they see fit.

Please see the image below to get a better idea of the set up.

Data Integration from SAP Ariba to SAP Datasphere

Once set up, you can schedule load jobs to have the data replicated into tables of an HDI container that is attached to your SAP Datasphere tenant. The SAP Datasphere models are based on top of these tables.

To get started, you can check out this detailed discovery mission that explains the set up and replication process in detail.

 

Content Documentation

Now where can you get more info on the analytics package?

For installing the packages on SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere side and getting started please check out the documentation. This will also give you an overview of all data models included.

You can find more details on the Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement content package in

SAP Analytics Cloud Content Documentation

SAP Datasphere Business Content Documentation

 

Hope you enjoyed reading this blog describing the new Value Analytics for SAP Ariba Procurement package. For feedback and enhancement ideas for the package, please reach out to us at  sap_analytics_cloud_content@sap.com

Also if you have questions and want to share with other users feel free to post a question in the community:

  • For SAP Analytics Cloud, please start here.
  • For SAP Datasphere, please start here

entry.title: SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations
entry.content:
Welcome to our 2408 Innovation blog, we are thrilled to share with you some exciting new features and improvements coming your way in the Procure to Order space. Let's dive into some of the key highlights that we hope will bring even more value to your procurement processes

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App - Purchase Requisition Approvals now available

With the role-based experience in the new SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers now have the convenience of easily finding requisitions in their to-do list, viewing line-item details, and then approving or rejecting them, all from within the mobile app. This eliminates the need to switch to the SAP Ariba web app for this task, offering a more streamlined and user-friendly experience.

  If you are eager for more information, you can browse this blog post on how to get ready for mobile shopping!

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Copy Additional RFQ Information to Requisitions

This enhancement means that Guided buying can now copy more information from RFQ events to purchase requisitions, so users and suppliers have all the necessary information for ordered items or services.

This means that critical fields on guided sourcing event line items like RFQ ID, Comments, Start and End Date, and Attachments are now seamlessly transferred from RFQ to Requisition, providing enhanced visibility and compliance for both buyers and suppliers through the source to order process.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Support for OData V4 APIs in the 2NV and 3EN Integration Scenarios

This is a vital feature and enabler for our S/4HANA or SAP S/4 HANA Cloud integration customers, as It provides an adoption path for the current state requisition integrations as the version 2 oData API has been deprecated.

This feature is an enabler for the other integration related features we cover in this blog for Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying (2NV) and Guided Buying for Central Procurement with SAP Ariba Buying (‏3EN‏).

For more technical information please see oData version 4 details, version comparison

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Ability to classify Attachments as Internal or External Documents in Guided Buying with SAP S/4HANA

In the Guided Buying Capability with SAP Ariba Buying integration scenarios Buyers can now indicate whether line-item level attachments are made visible to suppliers.

This critical indicator is sent to S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud to ensure that only those attachments flagged as external are shared with suppliers when a purchase order is distributed to the SAP Business Network. This enhances the experience for buyers and suppliers, bridging the current gap and providing enhanced control and clarity over document sharing.

 

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Improved Shipping Information for Guided Buying Requisitions with SAP S/4HANA

Previously, only basic shipping information was sent to SAP S/4HANA in the 2NV and 3EN integration scenarios where a delivery address was not defined as a plant, leaving potential gaps in key delivery information.

With this improvement, additional shipping details such as phone numbers, fax numbers, and email addresses are now included, facilitating more comprehensive and detailed communication capabilities between business entities ultimately helping users receive items or services more quickly.

This capability is enabled by the oData version 4 API previously mentioned, where additional fields in delivery information can now be leveraged. More supporting information for this integration scenario can be found here

 

We hope you are excited about these impactful new features and enhancements in SAP Ariba 2408 release. We encourage you to explore and adopt where relevant to your Ariba landscape, to further optimize your procurement processes and drive more value for your organization.

Please check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates as well as the following resources on our YouTube channels:

  • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
  • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2408 Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We’re thrilled to introduce several innovative features designed to enhance your procurement processes. These updates focus on improving visibility, efficiency, and integration across our SAP Ariba solutions. For those of you looking for a deeper dive into specific functionalities, we’ll also provide links to detailed blogs that explore a more comprehensive view for specific areas. Let's dive right in!

Master Data Overview in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (CIM-1428)

With the new Display Imported Master Data app, you can now view all master data along with replication details imported to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management from connected systems. This feature addresses the need for visibility over replicated master data, ensuring that you can validate and monitor data replication efficiently.

Extract Multiple PO References at Header Level (CIM-431)

Invoice processing just got a lot smoother. The Extract at Header Level Multiple PO References from Invoice feature utilizes SAP AI Business Services' Document Information Extraction to pull multiple PO reference values directly at the header level of an invoice. This advancement enhances automation and accuracy in handling invoices, helping you save time and reduce manual workload.

Duplicate Invoice Detection (CIM-220)

Enhance the reliability of your invoice processing automation with the Duplicate Invoice Detection feature. This function checks fields such as company code, invoicing party, external reference, and invoice date in draft invoices against existing invoices during editing or transfer. It automatically flags duplicates, preventing errors and potential fraud.

Purchase Requisition Approvals in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App (SAS-318)

Efficiency on the go! With the Support for Purchase Requisition Approvals in the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app, approvers can easily search for requisitions in their to-do list, view line-item details, and approve or reject them through a role-based user interface. This feature ensures that crucial approval workflows are accessible anytime, anywhere. Read more about how this feature can help streamline your mobile procurement processes in SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2408 Release Key Innovations

SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App The - Purchase Requisition Approvals screen

 

Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis (ARI-20913)

Gain critical insights into your suppliers' risks with the Supplier Risk Segmentation and Analysis feature. This tool allows you to view and analyze suppliers based on country/region and commodity risk, and easily export segmentation reports to a spreadsheet.

The Segmented View page with the new Create Engagement Request button

 

Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure (ARI-23844)

Customize your risk assessment with the Import Country Risk Score to Configure Risk Exposure feature. You now have the flexibility to use your own source of country risk score information, rather than solely relying on default data. Tailor your risk exposure configurations to better align with your organization's specific criteria and needs. For more information on SAP Ariba Supplier Management features, take a look at the SAP Ariba Supplier Management 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

Viewing Supplier Risk Level in SAP Ariba Category Management (CAT-5800)

Understanding supplier risk levels is vital for strategic category management. The new integration between SAP Ariba Category Management and SAP Ariba Supplier Risk allows category managers to view and assess supplier risk levels (Low, Medium, High). This visibility is essential for making informed decisions and strategizing effectively.

The new Risk Level available in the Category Suppliers page

Integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers (CAT-7893)

Enhance your cost structure analysis with the ability to integrate Cost Structure Data from External Market Intelligence Providers. Category management administrators can now incorporate external cost structure data directly into the Cost Structure tool within the strategy and plan document. This addition allows for more comprehensive and accurate strategy formulation. To delve deeper into this and other SAP Ariba Category Management, SAP Ariba guided sourcing, and SAP Ariba Contract Management, check out our SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2408 Release Key Innovations Preview

With these features, we are aiming to streamline your procurement processes, enhance risk management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. We encourage you to explore these exciting updates and implement them into your workflows to harness their full potential. Stay tuned for more updates by subscribing to myself and our other fantastic bloggers and take a look at the links below.

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2408 release
  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
  • Encourage customers to check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates.
  • Introduce customers to
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: OneSource integration on Ariba's purchase requisition
entry.content:
Hi,

I am considering using the OneSource integration for tax calculation and I had a few questions about its interactions with Ariba PRs. 

  1. Is there a way for the OneSource integration to run automatically on PRs created without needing to manually click the "Update tax" button?
  2. Does the OneSource integration work the same way for PRs that are created via web service?

entry.title: SAP Ariba Shopping App or SAP Ariba Procurement App? What fits best for me?
entry.content:
Hello Ariba Community,

This blog post aims to offer a comprehensive comparison of the functionality and product support provided by the SAP Ariba Shopping app and the SAP Ariba Procurement app, assisting you in determining which app is best suited for your specific procurement needs.

In essence, the SAP Ariba Shopping app, developed on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is a mobile native application that empowers employees and managers to navigate and shop from company catalogs. It is worth noting that the forthcoming 2411 update will introduce support for Level 1 punchout catalogs.

The shopping experience is comparable with shopping apps for consumers, therefore only minimal training is required to use the app. As shopping should be simple and fun you have certain restrictions on catalog item types and the ability of changing the purchase requisition. For those use cases, we recommend using the SAP Ariba Procurement app which is designed for procurement professionals and employees with advanced shopping needs such as ordering configurable items or doing an account split on item level.

Good news is that you can use both apps in parallel either for one user or for different user groups.

Let's first delve into the commercial conditions. The table below illustrates the Ariba products supported by each app:

 

Product                             

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

supported

supported

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

supported

supported

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

supported

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

 

supported

If your organization has subscribed any of the listed products, you are entitled to use the app without incurring any additional license costs. However, it is important to be aware of any potential limitations in how the apps are utilizing the supported Ariba products:

 

Product / Product Scope

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying with guided buying capability

Support for company catalogs and planned for 2411 punchout L1

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Support for company catalogs.

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Integration of guided buying with S/4 HANA or S/4 HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN scenario)

Both apps do not support requisitions when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN)

Both apps do not support requisitions created when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud  (2NV/3EN)

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

 

Support for:

  • New supplier creation
  • Supplier request tracking

 

Here is the functionality comparison of shopping for both apps:

 

Shopping Functionality

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

Shopping goods from company catalogs

supported

supported

Shopping goods from L1 punchout catalogs

supported

Support of BME catalog items

Edit item price

 

supported

Buy again

 

supported

Add account distributions

 

supported

Shop on behalf of another user

 

supported

Post Goods Receipt

 

supported

Change Purchase order

 

supported

 

Lastly, let's explore the roadmap for both applications. We are currently revising our roadmaps but one direction we can share is that we are thriving to enable Joule integration into our mobile app portfolio. As soon as we have clarity, I will share the roadmap items in a new blog post.

Enjoy shopping!

 

Michael Spiess

SAP Ariba Product Management

 

Further References:

  • New blog post about the latest release V1.12.0

  • Watch SAP Ariba Shopping App on Youtube

  • SAP Ariba Procurement roadmap for iOS and Android
  • Blog post: Getting started with SAP Ariba Shopping App in 7 steps
