entry.title: Integration of custom fields in standard Master Data Object using ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT program
entry.content:
Integration of custom fields in standard Master Data Object using ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT program

 

Ariba Procurement or Buying and Invoicing is often integrated with backend SAP ERP system. In such cases source of truth for master data is SAP ERP. CIG Addon provided by SAP has a master data export program named “ARBCIG_MASTER_DATA_EXPORT” which fetch the data from SAP convert it into a required format resulting in generation of csv files and then sends it to Ariba through CIG to upload the data in Ariba.

The master data export program is designed to extract the standard fields required in Ariba, any custom field added in Ariba is not supported using out of the box integration, however customization can be added in SAP ERP to send these custom fields from SAP ERP to Ariba if it exists in SAP ERP, which helps in reducing manual efforts of uploading the updated files in Ariba.

To understand how its done, let us consider an example where "Profit Center" and "Cost Center Category" fields are passed as custom fields in standard extract of cost center.

The first step is identifying the function module and structure that master data export program uses to export cost center information.

Here FM is “ARBCIG_COST_CENTER_EXPORT” and structure is “ARBCIG_COST_CENTER”. The structure has 6 fields as shown below.

Standard Structure "ARBCIG_COST_CENTER"

 

Now there are two ways, enhancement can be added in SAP ERP. Either utilize any existing fields to pass profit center and cost center category or create a custom field in the structure to pass these two fields.

Here we decided to add a custom field “ZPRCTR” with data element as “PRCTR” and “ZKOSAR” with data element as “KOSAR” to capture profit center and cost center category mapped to each cost center. Reason for creating a custom field is inability to use the existing fields.

Once the custom fields are added, structure looks as below.

Structure "ARBCIG_COST_CENTER" with Custom Fields

 

Once the structure is ready, next step is to write the logic to fill the data in “ZPRCTR” and “ZKOSAR” fields.

To do this “ARBCIG_MASTERDATA” BAdI can be used. Here we need to use method “IF_EX_ARBCIG_MASTERDATA~PUBLISH_COST_CENTER” to write the logic. Here logic to be written such a way that it updates the data in COST_CENTER_INFO and COST_CENTER_DEL elements so that data gets updated properly for incremental scenario as well.

Now execute the master data export program in test mode and observe the generated file. The file in AL11 looks as shown below.

Generated CostCenter.csv File

 

Here we can notice, additional columns ZPRCTR and ZKOSAR have been added in the file. Now it is important to validate the column name configured in Ariba. To do so, first ensure that, custom fields are already added in Ariba and then download the data dictionary to get the file column name of field profit center and cost center category in CostCenter.csv file.

Details of CostCenter.csv as per Data Dictionary

 

The highlighted rows in above image indicate complete details about custom fields, whereas highlighted cells indicate column name for the field Profit Center and Cost Center Category in CostCenter.csv file. Since here the column names are different than the structure’s field name it is necessary to add the data in “ARBCIG_FIELDMAP” table to replace ZPRCTR with "cus_ProftCenter" and ZKOSAR with "cus_CostCenterCategory".

 

Add below information in ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table,

Solution: AR Ariba Procurement

Structure: ARBCIG_COST_CENTER

Field SAP: ZPRCTR

Field Ariba: cus_ProfitCenter

 

Solution: AR Ariba Procurement

Structure: ARBCIG_COST_CENTER

Field SAP: ZKOSAR

Field Ariba: cus_CostCenterCategory

 

Once done ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table looks as shown below.

Updated Entries in "ARBCIG_FIELDMAP" Table

 

Now execute master data export once again and validate the file once again, notice column name has been updated as specified in ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table.

Generated CostCenter.csv with Updated Column Header

 

If no errors found execute the program without test mode. Download the cost center extract from Ariba to validate the custom data is successfully uploaded.

CostCenterExport.csv

 

ARBCIG_MASTERDATA BAdI and ARBCIG_FIELDMAP table are the two important tools SAP has provided to perform any customization to master data which is not available OOTB. Using this technique multiple customizations can be achieved as per business requirement.

entry.title: Discover SAP Business Technology Platform at Spend Connect Live
entry.content:
Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual are quickly approaching, and I can hardly contain my excitement! There you will be able to see the application of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) through expert sessions and customers stories. To prepare, here’s a summary of what SAP Business Technology Platform will be showing.

In the world of procurement, businesses need to find ways to make smarter spend decisions. According to a recent IDC research study sponsored by SAP, Overall, companies saw a 495% three-year return, taking only an average of six months to pay back on their investment by improving cross-organizational efficiencies, creating value through use of data, and more using SAP BTP.

Discover how SAP BTP can impact your Spend systems

If you’re new to SAP Business Technology Platform, Spend Connect Live has several sessions and resources that can help you discover SAP BTP. Our BTP101: Intro to SAP Business Technology Platform session can help you understand how SAP BTP optimizes your procurement processes. You can futher expand on the applications of SAP BTP and view use cases relating to procurement, business network, and supply chain in the BTP201: Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP session. For those interested in Concur, Streamline T&E management with integration of SAP and SAP Concur solutions will highlight how automation and integration between your Concur and ERP solutions can be done using SAP BTP.

Still have questions? You can visit us at the SAP Business Technology Platform Demo Pod on the show floor for additional demos and time with experts.

Hear directly from SAP customers how they benefit from SAP BTP

Now that you know what SAP BTP can do with spend solutions, you’re most likely now wondering how you can apply it to your business. Here are some notable customer use cases you can read, as well as some of the customer success stories being presented at Spend Connect Live.

Customer use case: Infosys, a leading provider improving supplier management

Infosys, an SAP customer, partner, and a global leader in technology consulting, has also improved supplier management. By using SAP BTP, Infosys were able to develop four custom applications for suppliers that allowed the integration of their many SAP solutions. With over 3,000 of their suppliers onboarded, they were able to decrease the onboarding time by 50%—all with one interface. Suppliers are now able to effectively use SAP Business Network for their needs, and Infosys can improve their supplier relations and processes.

Customer use case: Blue Diamond Growers, the biggest almond processor in the world optimizing key processes

Blue Diamond Growers  is the largest almond processor and marketer in the world, and are utilizing SAP BTP to support their over 3,000 suppliers and 1,800 employees by providing real-time data using SAP Analytics Cloud and automating manual processes using SAP Build Process Automation. Together with SAP Integration Suite, they are able to optimize key processes across procurement, inventory management, and quality control.

Spend Connect Live session: How Richemont enhanced supplier collaboration

At Spend Connect Live there are customer sessions highlighting how SAP BTP has elevated their procurement processes. At the session Growing supplier collaboration at Richemont to gain supply chain visibility, you can see firsthand how luxury goods group Richemont has contributed their improved performance to enhanced supplier collaboration using SAP BTP.

Spend Connect Live session: How Endress+Hauser digitized their asset lifestyle

Another customer session to note is Transforming asset collaboration at Endress+Hauser. At this session you can learn how the global leader of automations and measurement technology has digitized their asset lifecycle using SAP BTP, and they will be showcasing the benefits of using a platform along with SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration.  

Spend Connect Live session: Sony improves their cost management

Finally, technology conglomerate Sony will be presenting in Optimizing efficiency at Sony with SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP, where they will showcase how they utilized source-to-pay SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP to improve their cost management.

Learn more about SAP BTP for Spend and more virtually at Spend Connect Virtual

With SAP BTP being a unified cloud-based platform, it can optimize the processes for both companies and their suppliers, whether it be by improving visibility into procurement operations or integrating solutions for a seamless experience. As your business grows, you will need a platform that grows with you—Be sure to attend or watch the BTP101 and BTP201 sessions for the foundational knowledge of SAP BTP for spend. In anticipation for Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual you can learn more about SAP BTP for Spend at sap.com/btp-for-spend.

I’m looking forward to seeing you all at Spend Connect Live! Can’t make it in person? Register for Spend Connect Virtual here.

entry.title: Released now: Approve employees purchases with IF Design Award winning SAP Ariba Shopping App!
entry.content:
Dear Ariba Community,  

We are pleased to share the exciting news that employee shopping has become even more efficient with the launch of SAP Ariba Shopping version 1.12.0. In addition to this milestone, we are honored to announce that we have received the IF Design Award 2024 for our innovative and seamless buyer shopping experience!

With the latest release, users can now leverage the entire purchasing process through their mobile devices, from initiating the purchase request to receiving the goods at their designated location, using SAP Ariba Buying or SAP Ariba Buying, with guided buying capabilities. 

Let's take a closer look at how this workflow benefits an employee – let us take Colby from the latest YouTube video as an example - looking to order office equipment, such as a new phone. Whether utilizing SAP Ariba Buying or SAP Ariba Buying, with guided buying capabilities, Colby can easily start shopping by browsing through a selection of company catalogs within the app. Furthermore, company catalogs provide valuable supplementary information, including details on sustainability or even an augmented reality view of the items, if provided by the catalog provider. Additionally, with the upcoming Ariba release 2411, Colby will have access to Level 1 punchout catalogs, significantly expanding the range of available products for mobile shopping.

Once Colby has added the chosen items to his shopping cart and reviewed his selection, he can submit a purchase requisition to initiate the approval process within Ariba Buying. Based on the configured approval workflow, Colby's manager will receive an approval notification. This notification can be previewed on the manager's phone, and can be easily accessed with a single tab to proceed to the approval screen within the SAP Ariba Shopping app. From there, the manager can approve the purchase requisition, signaling the green light for the delivery of the requested items to the employee.  

We encourage you to explore the approval workflow within the demo mode of the SAP Ariba Shopping app. Should you wish to test the approval functionality with your Ariba system, please read this blog post or the onboarding guide at the SAP help documentation to get started with SAP Ariba Shopping. You find more details how to setup Ariba user mobile approver roles at this help page.

Please note that the approval functionality is still available within the SAP Ariba Procurement app. For a comprehensive comparison of the two apps, we recommend reading our new blog post.

Enjoy shopping!

 

Michael Spiess

SAP Ariba Product Management 

 

Further References:

  • New blog post about app comparison between SAP Ariba Shopping and SAP Ariba Procurement app
  • Download (iOS/Android) SAP Ariba Shopping app 
  • Watch SAP Ariba Shopping App on Youtube
  • Our roadmaps for iOS and Android.
  • Blog post: Gettings started with SAP Ariba Shopping App in 7 steps

entry.title: 2411 Early Release Series for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network releases
entry.content:
Sign up now for the 2411 Early Release Series

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Early Release Series
The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

  • Supplier Management & Risk
  • Procure-to-Pay
  • Source-to-Contract
  • Business Network

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications
Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Designated Support Contacts (DSC) and anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network  and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

Where to find out more details?
On demand replays of webcasts from completed quarterly releases can be consumed from Release Readiness Resources: Webcast Archive Page

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba community!

We're thrilled to announce some fantastic new features and enhancements for Q4 2024 across various SAP Ariba modules. These updates are designed to streamline your procurement processes, enhance supplier management, and provide deeper insights into supplier relationships. Let's take a high-level look at the most impactful innovations in this release.

For those of you wanting to explore specific functionalities in detail, we have provided links to dedicated blogs that dive deeper into each area. Let's take a look!

SAP Spend Control Tower

We're excited to share the latest development with you SAP Spend Control Tower. This new tool is designed to merge all your payment and supplier data into a single source of truth, enriched with AI-enabled spend classification and supplier insights. With intuitive dashboards and advanced machine learning models, it’s easier than ever to streamline our processes, cut procurement costs, manage supplier risks, and keep an eye on our diversity and sustainability goals.

Having a unified data source might just be the key to overcoming some of our biggest challenges. SAP Spend Control Tower takes the powerful analytics and reporting capabilities of the old SAP Ariba Spend Analysis solution and pushes them even further. If you're curious to learn more about how it could help us transform our spend management, I'd recommend checking out the full details on our SAP Spend Control Tower product page.

Spend overview dashboard in SAP Spend Control Tower

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance and Supplier Risk

Intelligent Recommendations Based on Supplier Risk Score

SAP Ariba Supplier Risk now offers intelligent recommendations for suppliers experiencing significant changes in risk scores. These new insights help buyers proactively identify and mitigate potential risks.

New recommendations section in the SAP Ariba Supplier Risk dashboard

SAP Business Network Certificate Information in the Supplier Profile Summary

The supplier profile summary now features added visibility into certificates maintained in SAP Business Network profiles. The new “Business Network Certificates” panel provides additional data points for supplier assessments, offering a comprehensive view directly from their SAP Business Network profile.

Business network certificates in the supplier profile summary

See the SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance & Supplier Risk 2411 Release: Key Feature Highlights blog for more information on these and other supplier management features

SAP Ariba Source to Contract

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP Ariba Category Management

We're excited to introduce enhancements to our Generative AI capabilities in SAP Ariba Category Management. You can now selectively generate articles for tools like Category Segmentation, Market Dynamics, and Cost Structure on demand, improving performance and reducing costs associated with producing AI-generated content.

Enhancements to Generative Artificial Intelligence in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to Create Category Strategy Details from the Develop Strategy and Plan App in SAP Ariba Category Management

Our new Category Strategy Details tool in the Develop Strategy and Plan app empowers category managers to create and manage multiple category-specific strategy details aligned with the overall business strategy.

Category strategy details in SAP Ariba Category Management

Ability to View, Analyze, and Manage Initiatives Across Categories in SAP Ariba Category Management

Manage your initiatives through the new Initiatives Overview app, providing comprehensive tools for tracking, optimizing, and visualizing data.

Initiatives overview in SAP Ariba Category Management

 

Create Optimization Scenarios for Multi-Round Bidding Events in Guided Sourcing

Generate optimization scenarios for multi-round bidding events, including editing constraints and updating scenarios with the latest data.

Enhancements to Grading and Scoring in Guided Sourcing

Improved grading features for guided sourcing, including offline grading for primary bids and rolled-up section level grading for alternate bids.

See the SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2411 Release Key Innovations Preview blog for more information on these and other source to contract features

SAP Ariba Procure to Order

Support for PunchOut Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

You can now purchase items from external supplier catalogs through Level 1 PunchOut catalogs directly from the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app. This enhancement broadens your product range for mobile shopping while ensuring compliance and approvals are managed through the normal Ariba checkout process.

View and Edit Requisition Line Items in SAP Ariba Shopping Mobile App

The SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app now provides detailed line item information in the requisition details screen and allows users to edit line items directly from the checkout screen. This update enhances flexibility and transparency in the mobile requisitioning process.

See the SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2411 Release Key Innovations blog for more information on these and other features

For further reading and resources, check out the links below and dive deeper into each specific innovation.

Resources:
    • Register for the Early Release Series for 2411 release
    • Resources:
      • Feature related materials
        • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
        • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
      • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
      • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network 
      • What’s New
    • Check out our content on youtube!
      • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
      • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2411 Release Key Innovations
entry.content:
Welcome to our 2411 Innovation blog, we are thrilled to share with you some exciting new features and improvements coming your way in the Procure to Order space. This time round we have a strong focus on enhanced capabilities and improved user experience across both guided buying and the SAP Ariba mobile shopping experience. We hope that these improvements will help drive more flexibility into the buying process, for both users in the office and on the move in today’s busy world.

SAP Ariba Shopping mobile App - Support for PunchOut Items

We are pleased to announce that you can now purchase items from external supplier catalogs through Level 1 PunchOut catalogs, directly from the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app. This feature was previously only available to desktop users and can now significantly broaden your product range for mobile shopping.

All necessary compliance and approvals are still maintained through the normal Ariba checkout process. This added capability provides greater flexibility, allowing purchases from a variety of external catalogs directly from your mobile device.

SAP Ariba Shopping mobile App - View and Edit Requisition Line Items

In addition to the mentioned PunchOut capability, in this release we have also enhanced the SAP Ariba Shopping mobile app to offer more visibility and control over your purchases. Users can now view detailed line item information in the requisition details screen and make necessary edits to line items directly from the checkout screen.

This update ensures that the mobile requisitioning process is as flexible and transparent as possible, allowing for simple review and adjustments on the go.

For further information on SAP Ariba Shopping app please see the recent blog

SAP Ariba Buying, guided buying capability - Additional Supplier Contact Information on Multi-Line RFQ

To facilitate better communication, we’ve added more supplier contact information to the RFQ process. Previously, RFQs included only the supplier's name and email address, which sometimes led to communication issues in identifying the right person from the supplier.

Now, the contact information will also feature phone numbers. Additionally, RFQs can be directed to the primary contact and Bid/Proposal Manager only if required, ensuring that RFQs reach the intended contacts.

This improvement is aimed at increasing accuracy and efficiency in the RFQ process.

SAP Ariba Procurement mobile App - Support for Central Receiving

In this release we have also enhanced the SAP Ariba Procurement mobile App, we have now introduced the ability to perform central receiving tasks directly from the SAP Ariba Procurement mobile app. Previously, this was possible only through the web application which was not always convenient for central receiving on the move. The app also now displays the approval flow for receipts.

These enhancements provide more flexibility and convenience, allowing users to manage crucial receiving tasks and maintain transparency over the approval process while on the go.

For a holistic comparison of SAP Ariba Mobile app capabilities please see this blog

We're confident that these enhancements will provide significant business value and improve your overall SAP Ariba experience. Stay tuned for more exciting updates!

  • Register for the Early Release Series for 2411 release
  • Feature related materials SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
  • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
  • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
  • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
  • What’s New

Please check out the other blogs, and subscribe to the authors for latest updates aswell as the following resources on our YouTube channels:

  • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
  • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: How to Use Splunk for Data Analysis in SAP Ariba Applications: A Beginner's Guide. Part 1
entry.content:
Introduction

Welcome to the comprehensive beginner's guide on using Splunk for Data Analysis in SAP Ariba Application. This guide is divided into several parts, each providing detailed instructions for understanding the main features and functionality of the Splunk tool. Without further ado, let's embark on the journey to enhance data analysis capabilities in SAP Ariba.

What is the Splunk?

Splunk is an essential data analysis tool that plays a crucial role in aggregating data for analysis and providing logs. Particularly for SAP Ariba, it allows the monitoring of every aspect of the application processes, specifically  to procurement and supply chain. By doing so, it can help in tracking and analyzing transaction data, monitoring system performance, identifying security threa, and gaining visibility into operational inefficiencies. Moreover, Splunk provides the capability to proactively identify and resolve issues, optimize processes, and make data-driven decisions to improve overall efficiency and effectiveness.

One of the key features of Splunk is that it is comprised of several different applications. These apps essentially consist of a collection of files containing data inputs, UI elements, and knowledge objects. This flexibility with different apps allows for various use cases and user roles to coexist within a single Splunk instance.

 Splunk typically retains logs for a period ranging from 90 to 390 days, depending on the data center and the specific index. This ensures that the data is accessible for analysis and reference within the desired timeframe.

The Splunk access URL is determined by the SAP Ariba component and data center.

List of all Splunk and Inspector Links for every component by datacenter

It is important to note that the access to Splunk is only possible with Safeguard account and only SAP employees have access to Splunk tool.

 

Key components of the Splunk platform

Indexer.

  • Processes machine data from any source
  • Parses data by an organized source type (index)
  • This raw log data is broken up into single events
  • Events are stored by the index so they can be searched
  • Run the actual search done by user

Search Head

  • Allows users to use Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL) to search indexed data
  • Distributes the search to the Indexers
  • Extracts field value pairs from events to the user
  • Can extract additional fields (i.e. regex) or transform data (i.e. stats commands) for results before it is returned to end user without changing the underlying data
  • Provide tools for reports/dashboards/visualizations

Forwarder

  • Installed on servers where data originates
  • Primary way data is supplied to indexers for indexing

    Splunk’s Architecture

    The Cloud Health 2.0 App

    The Cloud Health 2.0 App is one of the key tool within the Splunk platform, utilized by various SAP Ariba teams for analyzing application log data. This app provides support and development teams with the capability to examine log data from any SAP Ariba component

    One of the distinguishing features of the Cloud Health 2.0 App is its multitude of customized, pre-extracted and calculated fields, such as AribaThread, AribaPrealm, and Community. These fields are exclusively available within the Cloud Health 2.0 app knowledge object, enabling users to leverage them in search queries.

    In addition to field extractions, the knowledge objects within the Cloud Health 2.0 App encompass lookups, fields, and alerts, providing a comprehensive toolkit for effective analysis and monitoring of log data.

    The app's value is underscored by the fact that utilizing these specific fields in the default search app of Splunk – the search and reporting app – yields no results unless the corresponding knowledge object exists within it.

    Another benefit of the Cloud 2.0 app for users is that all dashboards and alert knowledge objects are shared across global data centers.

    Main page

    The opening of the cloud health signifies the importance of the application, which is prominently positioned at the top of the black Splunk menu bar. This feature is accessible from any Splunk application, providing users with ease of access. Within the aforementioned bar, users can find the application chooser, which allows for seamless switching between different apps. This centralized location serves as a convenient hub for app navigation.Furthermore, each application contains its own specific navigation features within the bar, ensuring a tailored user experience for each individual app.

    If someone were to wish to run a simple, ad hoc search, they could do so by clicking on the search button. This action would subsequently open the search screen in a new tab, displaying a page with the desired results. Users can then input their search criteria into the search bar, beginning with "index equals," followed by the specified index. In some cases, users may also be searching for a specific string of text.

    Upon running the search and obtaining results, a bar chart depicting the event timeline will be displayed. This chart effectively illustrates the distribution of events across time intervals. The specific timing of these intervals will adjust dynamically depending on the length of the chosen time frame for the search.

    Each individual result is considered an event, with different fields and values attributed to each one. For example, the "host" field is accompanied by a corresponding value. Additionally, there are various job functions located on the top right-hand side of the page.

Search example

The time zone displayed for specific events is based on user preferences and can be adjusted according to individual needs. To do so, simply click on your name, navigate to the preferences section, then select the global settings and change the time zone as desired. This allows users to ensure events and appointments are accurately reflected in their own local time.

Timezone

Search Assistant

The search assistant is a helpful tool that offers various selections and options for completing a search. Users have the ability to select a term from a list, continue typing, and choose between default, compact, or full view. To access or deactivate the search assistant, users must click on their name, navigate to preferences, select SPL editor, and then the search assistant.

Preferences

The compact mode provides command information as users type and displays the last five queries for quick selection. In full mode, the display provides more information and includes more text as well as examples. However, the choice of which view to use ultimately depends on the user's preference.

Search Assistant Compact Mode

Search Assistant Full mode

The next topic for consideration is the selection of time in the Search feature. When it comes to selecting the time, there are several different options available. One option is to select the time from a time picker. Another option is that real-time search is only allowed for Power/Admin users. Additionally, time can be defined in the search query using various options such as "earliest" or "latest." Time notation is also available, with "s" representing seconds, "m" representing minutes, "h" representing hours, "d" representing days, "w" representing weeks, "mon" representing months, and "y" representing years.

 Selecting Time in Search

 

In order to view a specific subset of events using the search feature, users can click on a particular bar. This action filters the search results to display only the events related to the selected bar. To clear the filter, simply clicking on any part of the white space in the bar will remove it.

Search Example

Controlling the jobs allows for various options such as pausing a job while it is still running and stopping a job if needed. Additionally, the default duration for active search jobs is 10 minutes, but they can be shared with others via a URL that remains active for seven days. The search results can be downloaded in different formats such as CSV, JSON, XML, or raw text. There are three different search modes available: fast, which only returns default field data or any required fields for the search; verbose, which returns a large amount of data and may impact search performance if used for large queries; and smart, which recommends field discovery for searches and adds new fields if Splunk determines there is enough data to add them. The smart mode also does not provide event or field data for stats searches. Overall, these options provide flexibility and control in managing search jobs and their results.

 Controlling Jobs

In order to access previous job and search history, users can click on the activity section and view jobs by the owner application or status. The search history can be found at the bottom of the search page, where users can expand and view their previous searches. This feature is particularly useful for users who may have forgotten to save a query they ran a few days ago. The search history page is designed to retain data for up to 30 days, allowing users to access any searches conducted within that timeframe.

History

Conclusion

Having finished the basic overview of the Splunk tool and its main features for SAP Ariba data analysis.  On next parts will continue to dive to this tool and will talk about Dashboards and Alerts & Schedulated Reports. . The aim of this guide is to be helpful and aid in making comprehensive analysis of any Ariba issues on a daily basis. Should you have any questions or need further assistance, do not hesitate to reach out.

SAP Ariba Sourcing  SAP Ariba Invoice Management  SAP Ariba Spend Analysis SAP Ariba Supplier Risk #splunk

entry.title: FAQs regarding Receiving process in Ariba
entry.content:
Receiving is the process of confirming that the goods or services ordered from a supplier have been delivered and are satisfactory. It is an essential function in the P2P process, both from the audit and compliance perspectives. It ensures that the enterprise is paying correctly to the suppliers based on the actual deliveries received. It also provides a mechanism for returning undesired items. 

Let's dive deep into the Receiving function and understand it using FAQs.

Receiving Methods

Q - I can I do Receiving in Ariba?

A -  Please see below the receiving methods available in Ariba.

  1. Automatic receiving - Enables Ariba to create receipts automatically based on specific configurations.
  2. Manual Receiving - In this method, the users manually create a receipt that indicates the extent to which the order was received. Users can also revise the quantity/amount downward by entering negative values, as you might reject some items. 
  3. Line-level receiving: In this method, receipts are created in the ERP system and imported into Ariba as proof of records.

Manual Receiving Method -

Q - Who can do manual receiving in Ariba ?

A - Manual receiving in Ariba is done through any of the two Manual Receiving Types

  1. Desktop receiving—In this type, the Casual user (who must have the Purchasing User group assigned) can receives the goods and create Receipts.
  2. Central Receiving – Receiving is done by the team/member who must have a Receiving Manager or Receiving Agent group assigned in Ariba. 

Q - I don't want my casual users to be bothered about receiving all the times, Can I leverage my Central receiving team when required ?

A - Of course, you can formulate a strategy for what categories you want casual users to receive and what should be received by the central team. The default configuration supports central receiving based on commodity code. Users who are members of a specific group receive goods that have a specific commodity code. For example, the IT department is in charge of receiving all IT-related items and then distributing them to the end users.

Q - Can I receive multiple orders simultaneously when manually receiving?

A - Yes, you can receive multiple orders simultaneously, provided the orders don't require additional data entry. However, If any order requires asset or other data entry in the receipt, you're notified to enter individual receipts for those items.

Q - Can I create multiple receipts for a PO?

A - Yes, you can create multiple receipts for a PO, this is called partial receiving. If the order is partially received you must create additional receipts as you receive additional line items.

Q - Can I create one Receipt for multiple PO in Ariba?

A - While partial receiving, creating multiple receipts to fulfill a PO, is supported in Ariba; a single receipt for multiple purchase orders is not allowed.

Q - WHAT are the different types of manual receipts that can be created in Ariba?

Below are the Manual Receiving Types available in Ariba

  1. Manual Receive by Quantity - Here user manually receive the item by quantity. This is the Default receiving type
  2. Manual Receive by Amount - Here user manually Receive by amount in base currency such as USD.

Q - How do I determine the right manual receiving type for my organization?

Organizations generally use quantity-based receiving for goods items and amount-based receiving for service items. 

Q - Do you have a workflow diagram for Manual Receiving for better understanding?

Automatic Receiving Method -

Q - How do I decide if my company should go for automatic receiving or receive the goods manually?

A - Good question, Let me share with you the below use cases that drives the use of automatic receiving of goods -

  1. Use auto-receive to eliminate time spent on data entry for low-value receipts
  2. Use auto-receive to reduce the risk of pending payments for small invoices awaiting a manual receipt.

Q - Can I do manual receiving for a commodity marked as auto-receive to make corrections?

A - Yes, you can and this is a valid business scenario. To achieve this, when you manually receive a line item initially configured for auto-receiving, Ariba converts the receiving type for that line item to a manual receipt type. Note, this doesn't impact the receiving type configured for that commodity in other PO.

Q - Can I change the PO or cancel it entirely after it's auto-received?

A - Yes, you can change/cancel an auto-received PO. To do so, you need to manually create negative receipts for the PO, which converts it to a manually received PO. Once done, you can choose to either change or cancel such purchase orders.

Q - What are the different ways I can configure automatic receiving in Ariba?

A - Receipts are automatically created based on auto-receiving configuration. Ariba offers the below auto-receiving types options -

  1. Auto Receive below a specific amount: Items of lower dollar value will be auto-received. If the receipt value exceeds the specific dollar amount, someone must manually receive the item.
  2. Auto-receipt on due date: Ariba automatically generates a receipt on the order due date.
  3. Auto-receipt on order date: Ariba automatically generates a receipt when the item is ordered.
  4. Auto-receipt on invoice or charge reconciliation: Ariba automatically generates a receipt when either an IR document or a charge reconciliation document is generated.

Q - How do I know my order's due date, required for automatic receiving?

A - Auto-receipt on the due date is the ideal configuration for receiving catalog items automatically, where the due date is calculated based on catalog item lead time. For non-catalog items, the default lead time parameter is zero in Ariba.

Line-level Receiving Method -

Q - Some users in my organisation are in Ariba as well as the ERP. Can they receive goods partially in Ariba and ERP?

A - Yes, Line-level receiving method in Ariba enables users to receive items in ERP and/or Ariba. Receipts can be handled for a PO using a combination of manual and imported receipts. Ariba offers below options, which enterprises can choose depending on the business rule and the use case

  1. line-level receiving in the enhanced mode -
    1. Users can create receipts in Ariba by importing receipt data from external ERP systems.
    2. Application.Receiving.AllowReceivingByUserInterfaceAndReceiptImportEnhanced parameter (set by SAP) must be enabled to create receipts in multiple ways for a PO line.
  2. line-level receiving, without the enhanced mode -
    1. Users can create receipts for a PO line item either in Ariba or by importing receipts, but not both.
    2. A member of the Customer Administrator group sets this parameter in the ICM - Application.Receiving.AllowReceivingByUserInterfaceAndReceiptImport ;
      1. When set to Yes, receivers can create receipts either in the buying solution or by using imported receipts, but not both.
      2. When set to No, the way receivers can create receipts depends on how the first receiving transaction is processed.

Q - As Ariba Admin, how do I configure Receiving process in Ariba ?

  1. Auto-receiving can be enabled based on item's supplier part number or commodity code.
  2. Configure threshold amounts for specific commodities and supplier part numbers so that the items are auto-received only if the PO line is under the specified threshold amount.
  3. Ariba also offers flexibility in determining the Receiving types based on specific fields in the requisition, including custom fields.
  4. Tolerance settings are used to control over- and under-receiving orders.

Q - How can I make an item be received automatically for low values while manually receiving high values for the same category?

A - Amount thresholds determine whether PO items are auto-received or manually-received. Ariba  automatically receives PO lines with an amount less than or equal to the threshold amount.

There are two ways to configure amount thresholds for auto-receiving:

  1. You can configure auto-receiving amount thresholds for order lines based on commodity code or supplier part number. This way, the amount threshold for one commodity code or part number can be different from the threshold for another commodity code or part number.
  2. SAP Ariba Customer Support can configure site-wide amount thresholds at the order and line levels. These site-wide thresholds apply to all lines and orders, regardless of commodity code or part number.

Q - I want to set the receiving type for a commodity as auto-receiving; however, I want to configure manual receiving for a particular part number belonging to that commodity. Is it possible?

A-We understand that such business requirements are often required. Hence, Ariba offers this functionality: for a particular item, the receiving type associated with the part number takes precedence over the receiving type associated with the commodity code.

Example : You can configure Notebook computers (UNSPSC# 43211503) for auto-receiving. However, you can add an additional configuration that Part # starting with APPLE% always require manual receiving despite it belonging to the 'Notebook computers' commodity.

Under & Over Receiving

Q - Can I receive more than the quantity/amount ordered?

Yes, Buyers can receive more or less than the ordered quantity, depending on business needs. Ariba provides the under-receiving and over-receiving functionality.

In general, the quantities and amounts on a receipt are expected to match those on the purchase order. However, sometimes you may receive fewer items or for a lesser amount than what was specified on the purchase order (under-receiving), or you may receive more items or for a higher amount than on the purchase order (over-receiving). This is possible for different use cases such as price fluctuation, suppliers sending additional quantities to makeup for any damages etc.

Q - Is there a limit to how much I can under/over-receive?

Yes, such limits are governed by the receiving tolerance configured in Ariba. The purchase order will be considered fully received if you submit receipts for quantities and/or amounts within the configured tolerance limits.

Tolerance can be defined based on -

  1. Absolute Quantity
  2. Value (Line Item Amount)
  3. Percentage of Qty/Amount

Q: Do I need to meet all the above-listed tolerances for receiving, or is meeting any one is enough?

Your site can be configured so that absolute and relative tolerances must be met for a receipt to be fully received, or either one of the tolerance settings must be met for the receipt to be complete.

Here is a very good example to explain this - Example for under & over Receiving 

Q—So far, so good; however, in a complex business scenario where a company has several subsidiaries that functions as independent purchasing units, the receiving behavior for such subsidiaries may differ from that of the parent company. How do we deal with such a difference in receiving tolerance requirements?

A - To facilitate such differences at the purchasing unit level, you can configure the receiving tolerances specific to each purchasing unit. The Purchasing unit hierarchies for receiving tolerances are not supported, hence, please configure the tolerance for each PU; otherwise, the receiving tolerances that are configured at your site level will be applicable for such unconfigured purchasing units.

Receipt Information Sent to Suppliers

Q - How do I inform my suppliers that I have received the items?

A - You can configure your buying solution to send receipt information to SAP Business Network, allowing suppliers to view the approval status of receipts and receipt information, such as the number of items accepted or rejected.

A member of the Customer Administrator group sets this parameter in ICM - Application.Receiving.SendReceiptsToAribaNetwork

Q - Why does SAP recommend to send receipt information to my suppliers?

A - With receipt information available on SAP Business Network, suppliers can view the status of receipt transactions and create invoices based on actual receipts. This enables quicker transaction closure and reduces receipt-related exceptions while reconciling invoices.

Asset Data on Receipts

Q - I order a lot of items which are of type Assets. Can I receive Assets in Ariba?

A - Yes, Ariba supports Asset-receiving functionality. Asset receiving in Ariba allows asset data (for example, asset serial number and tag number) to be added to the receipt line items identified as assets.

Q - How do I configure categories/part # as Assets in Ariba?

A - A receipt line item is identified as an Asset based on the Part Number or Commodity Code. If the asset data feature is enabled on your site, adding asset details to the corresponding items in the receipt is mandatory before it is approved.

The Import Receiving Types By Commodity Code data import task lets you specify which commodity codes require asset data information while The Import Receiving Types By Part Number data import task lets you specify which part numbers require asset data information.

You can add asset data in one of the following ways (Help link included) :

  1. Manually, for each received item – The procedure is described here. Specifying Asset Data Manually for Received Items
  2. By importing asset data from an Excel template. The procedure is described here: Specifying Asset Data Using an Excel Template.
  3. Automatically based on the value set in the Application.Procure.DefaultAssetDataSize (Set by SAP Ariba Support) parameter. Details can be seen here -  DefaultAssetDataSize.

Q - Who can add the asset data to the receipts before they are sent to the ERP for processing?

Either the requester or any one of the approvers must ensure that asset data is specified before the receipts are fully approved and sent to the ERP.

Typically, the main receiver adds asset data at the time of receiving, but this data may not always be accurate. To ensure accurate information is provided, you can add Asset Receiver group to the approval flow.

The asset receiver can then review the specified asset information, change it if necessary, and approve the receipt.

Additional Information :

In the default configuration, when asset data is enabled for receiving, an asset receiver or the customer administrator is automatically added at the end of the receipt approval process. This ensures that asset data is specified before the receipt is fully approved.

An exception is raised if the asset receiver tries to approve the receipt without adding this asset data.

Q - How can I add Receivers in Ariba to make them responsible for receiving?

A - Designating the "Receiver" in Ariba can be done using the Approval process

  • The person or Group responsible for  doing the receiving against a Purchase Order (manual receiving) is designated through a Receipt "Approval" ruleset
  • The first "Approver" in the ruleset is the  person who needs to do the receiving (complete the required fields to submit the Receipt)
  • The Receipt "Approvals" have the same capabilities as another approval rule, including leveraging Lookup Tables.
  • It is not common to require additional Receipt approvals other than for asset-receiving

Q - How can I map receiving types with commodity codes and amount thresholds?

The Import Receiving Types By Commodity Code data import task lets you associate receiving types with commodity codes and amount thresholds. If necessary, you can also configure custom conditions to determine the receiving type based on the fields on the requisition.

Import Receiving Types By Commodity Code reads data from the CSV file ReceiptByCommodityCode.csv.

Q - How can I map receiving types with Part numbers?

The Import Receiving Types By Part Number data import task associates receiving types and amount thresholds with part numbers. Import Receiving Types By Part Number reads data from the CSV file named ReceiptByPartNumber.csv.

Thank you for taking the time to read the blog. I hope it helps you build a business case for choosing Ariba for your receiving process. If you have any questions about the receiving process that aren’t covered here, please feel free to send them to me. I’d be happy to add them to the FAQs for everyone’s benefit.

entry.title: 2502 Early Release Series for SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network releases
entry.content:
Sign up now for the 2502 Early Release Series

Follow this blog to receive notification of new series as soon as registration opens.

SAP strives to equip our customers and partners with valuable release readiness resources for each SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network quarterly release.

Early Release Series

The SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network Early Release series of webcasts cover features planned for the upcoming quarterly release.  This platform, available to customers and partners, has a clear mission: deliver an interactive forum to engage with SAP product experts providing simple, direct answers to your most pressing questions.  There is a different session for each product area with content tailored for the specific audience:

  • Business Network
  • Supplier Management & Risk and Procure to Pay
  • Source to Contract

If you want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts, we encourage you to participate in these informative sessions.  Register now!

Quarterly Release Communications

Notice of upcoming quarterly releases will be communicated to Buyer customers three weeks prior to the release date.  Suppliers receive notice two weeks prior.  Releases are communicated via email notification (look for subject line starting with: Advance Release Notice).

Anyone from your organization that has chosen to subscribe to product notifications will receive these announcements. 

The release calendar for SAP Business Network and release calendar SAP Ariba Applications identify release timing several quarters in advance.

 Where to find out more details?

On demand replays of webcasts from completed quarterly releases can be consumed from Release Readiness Resources: Webcast Archive Page

entry.title: SAP Ariba 2502 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Community! We are thrilled to present the latest features and enhancements from our 2502 release, focused on streamlining procurement processes, enhancing configuration flexibility, and boosting compliance. Let's delve deeper into the key innovations transforming your procurement and sourcing landscape:

Intelligent Configuration Manager Enhancements

Feature Toggle Management (PLICM-4237) Administrators now have the autonomy to manage select feature toggles directly, streamlining the process without needing to rely on SAP. This allows for a more agile testing and deployment cycle, promoting smoother transitions from test to production environments.

Feature Toggle Management

Please note this blog has been edited to remove reference to Configuration Bundles Creation (PLICM-4464) and Site Configuration Migration (PLICM-4465) as these features have been pushed to a future release.

Highlights from Procure-to-Order

Revamped Item Details Page (GB-24673) The item details page has been redesigned for a superior user experience, featuring advanced pricing information, multiple item images, and expanded supplier insights. A new section now gives insights into supplier diversity and risk attributes, aligning with your sustainability goals.

Revamped Item Details Page

 Spend Consumption Reports (CP-29451)  Access to Spend Consumption reports provides comprehensive tracking of PO and Non-PO spends, allowing organizations to enhance compliance and optimize spend management. These reports can be customized to fit billing cycles, offering flexibility and deeper insights.

Price Validation on Import (CP-29879) Ensure accuracy with automated price validation checks for requisition imports. The feature streamlines processing by flagging price discrepancies immediately, promoting efficient requisition handling and compliance with budget standards.

For further details on these innovations, take a look at our SAP Ariba Procure to Order 2502 Release Key Innovations

Highlights from Source to Contract

Travel & Expense dashboard, presenting information from Concur (ARBTS-56) The newly introduced Travel & Expense Dashboard integrates seamlessly with SAP Concur, offering detailed insights and reporting on expense types and payment status across multiple currencies. This allows for informed decision-making and spend optimization in global contexts.

Travel and Expense Dashboard

 

 


AI-Assisted Category Strategy (CATMAN-467) Leverage AI to generate strategic category recommendations, efficiently digitizing and analyzing category data and market dynamics. This supports effective strategy formulation and alignment with business objectives.

AI-Assisted Category Strategy

 

 

Supplier Preferencing Matrix (ARBCAT-726) A strategic tool for evaluating supplier relationships based on business value, aiding in better engagement, negotiation, and partnership opportunities, which ultimately bolsters procurement performance.

Supplier Preferencing Matrix

 

 

Operational Reporting APIs for SAP Ariba Category Management (CATMAN-468) Harness the power of APIs to access operational data from SAP Ariba Category Management, supporting robust data analysis through third-party tools to drive informed decision-making.

Support for Large-Capacity Events in Guided Sourcing (SS-58288) large-capacity RFP events allows you to manage complex sourcing workflows involving thousands of line items and hundreds of suppliers, thereby improving the scalability and reach of your sourcing initiatives.

For further details on these innovations and more, take a look at our SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2502 Release Key Innovations

Highlights from Central Invoice Management

Automatic Reprocessing of Invoices (CIM-657) Automate invoice reprocessing for missing receipts, providing better visibility and reducing the necessity for manual interventions, thus increasing efficiency.

Automatic Reprocessing of Invoices with Missing Receipts

 

Data Center US10 Deployment (CIM-1918) Establish a hosting presence in the United States for a broader range of options in managing access to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, offering flexibility aligned with business needs.

Peppol Network Integration (CIM-1159, CIM-212 - CIM-1150) Enable invoice receipt through the Peppol network for several countries, including New Zealand, Australia, Finland, Belgium, and Luxembourg, enhancing international invoicing capabilities.

XML File Processing with API (CIM-1816)  Support XML files in Supplier Invoices and Document Extraction API, improving data extraction from invoices for streamlined processing and increased accuracy, including support for electronic invoices like Germany's XRechnung.

 

Enhanced Approval Workflow Conditions (CIM-1585) Introduce new conditions for approval workflows, adding flexibility in handling invoices based on Purchase Orders and other criteria to align closely with business processes.

Parallel Workflow Support (CIM-1711) Support parallel workflows for supplier invoices, enabling simultaneous approval and information requests, which enhances workflow efficiency and reliability.

Document Information Extraction Templates (CIM-1942) Utilize templates to improve data field extraction from invoices, ensuring enhanced accuracy and reducing manual entry errors.

Use Document Information Extraction Templates

 

New India Data Center Support (CES-1088)

Localized Connectivity: New SAP Ariba customers in India can connect to the latest India data center, offering improved performance and optimized data localization for enhanced regulatory compliance. Note that existing customers cannot migrate their data to the new data center.

These innovations aim to significantly enhance your procurement and sourcing capabilities, leading to improved efficiency, data-driven insights, and strengthened supplier relationships.

Stay connected with our community for more insights and updates:

Register for the Early Release Series for 2502 release

  • Resources:
    • Feature related materials
      • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
      • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
    • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
    • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
    • What’s New
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ

Make sure to check out our other blogs and subscribe to all of our authors to keep up to date with the latest and greatest innovations!

entry.title: What's New in 2502 - Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network
entry.content:
The Readiness@Scale team is excited to announce that the first release of 2025 is coming up quickly! In 2024, the Release Navigators were accessed over 90,000 times to prepare for SAP releases. Make sure to visit the 2502 edition of the Release Navigator for Spend Management and Business Network on January 27th for updated release content to ensure a success release. This update will showcase the latest content to keep you informed and prepared for the newest innovations. Access all essential resources in one convenient location for all of Spend Management and Business Network including What’s New Viewer, Release Blogs, Webcasts, Release Calendars, Blogs, Tutorial Videos, and more. Click the link below to easily and efficiently prepare for the 2502 Release!

  • SC-22437 Decommissioning of Next Gen UI for CLID in SAP Ariba Contracts: This feature removes the ability to use the Next Gen UI for CLID in SAP Ariba Contracts. Customers must use the classic user interface, available by default, to manage Contract Line Items Documents (CLID) in SAP Ariba Contracts.
  • Depreciation for weak TLS 1.2 cipher suite connections and enable support for TLS 1.3: Starting Friday, January 24, 2025, there will be a change to the SAP Ariba applications and SAP Business Network supported cipher suite connections.  On this date, new TLS cipher suite connection security standards will be implemented; support for weak TLS 1.2 connections will end and the support of TLS 1.3 connections will begin.

Making Your Impact

At SAP, we greatly appreciate your input and suggestions. You are invited to submit feedback and suggest topics to be covered through our Topic Request & Feedback Survey. This survey provides you with an opportunity to provide insights on your experience with the release navigator for Spend Management and Business Network, as well as suggestions on how we can further enhance your release experience. Additionally, we encourage you to share your feedback, thoughts, or questions in the comment section below. 

Staying Connected

Follow the Spend Management and Business Network and ReadinessS@Scale community tags, and Ryan Russman to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

entry.title: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
Hello procurement folks,

please welcome SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Release 2502. In the fast-paced world of business, a smooth procurement process can make all the difference. These days, that efficiency comes down to having the right software. In this post, we'll take a look at the latest procurement updates and explore how these advancements can simplify your operations, cut costs, and ultimately boost your bottom line. Watch the video and find the top highlights of this release.

For other releases please see here. Check out all demos in a playlist here.

Operational Procurement

  • Punchout Capability for Supplier hosted Catalogs
  • Enabling partner functions in the Purchase Order API

SAP Ariba Central Procurement

  • Mass Change of Price Scales in Central Purchase Contracts
  • Support of Enhanced Limit in Central Supplier Confirmation
  • Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement (6BJ)

Invoice Management

  • Alternative Reconciliation Account
  • My Inbox: CDS Based Custom Attributes for Supplier Invoices

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

  • Use of Document Information Extraction templates
  • Supporting different start and step conditions (amount, PO-based, credit memo)
  • Automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts

Operational Procurement

Punchout Capability for Supplier hosted Catalogs

The base edition of SAP Ariba Buying delivers a smart and simple purchasing experience designed to make it easier for S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers to jumpstart their path to best-in-class procurement performance, paving the way for broader digital transformation whenever your business is ready.

SAP Ariba Buying creates a simple, smart, and elegant purchasing experience that increases user engagement for indirect material procurement.  The newly added punch-out functionality enables buyers to directly access and purchase goods/services from catalog's maintained and hosted by the suppliers. This allows buyers to quickly add items from external stores to their shopping cart and initiate a purchase request that follows the approval process configured. Access real-time information on pricing and availability from external stores during the buying process. 

You wonder how this is being set up? The punchout catalogs are configured in the configuration section of SAP Ariba Buying, Base Edition via Manage Shopping Sites , here you have to configure every details of the punchout catalog like authentication, URL etc. For more information, see: Adding a Supplier Store

Enabling partner functions in the Purchase Order API

Partner functions enable to manage the relationships of different suppliers. Certain partner functions be maintained in the business partner (supplier), which can be copied to the procurement documents under each respective Purchasing Organization. With this innovation, the Purchase Order OData V4 API can be used to send the partner function fields. Use the OData V4 API 'CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001' to create, update, read, and delete partner functions in the purchase order header. For more information, see: Purchase Order (CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001) and Purchase Order (OData V4).

back to top

SAP Ariba Central Procurement

Mass Change of Price Scales in Central Purchase Contracts

We increase the flexibility and usability for mass managing of central purchase contracts. This innovation is an extension of the existing mass change capability for central purchase contracts through offline Microsoft Excel upload. SAP Ariba Central Procurement now also supports the update and creation of price scale values for condition types configured for price scale maintenance. Furthermore, purchasers can now choose to only upload changes in preparation mode and releasing those through a later mass update. Support for additional fields by the offline Excel upload including:

  • Numerator and denominator on item level
  • Planned delivery duration on item level
  • Item category on item level
  • Unit of measurement conversion on price-condition level
  • Incoterms classification and location 1 on distribution level 

For more information, see: Mass Changes to Central Purchase Contracts

Support of Enhanced Limit in Central Supplier Confirmation

With this release, you can now create a central supplier confirmation for a centrally managed purchase order with E (Enhanced Limit) in the 'Manage Central Supplier Confirmations' app. You can add, view and change confirmation items of item categories Standard (blank), lean services and E (Enhanced Limit).  The Enhanced Limit is an item category in a purchase order that can be used to order unplanned services and that is defined by a maximum amount.

Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement (6BJ)

We are happy to speed up procurement processes with the integration of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SAP Business Network through SAP Integration Suite. Central purchasers can now create central purchase orders in the central procurement hub system by pooling purchase orders from connected systems, such as SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA or ECC. The purchase orders are sent to SAP Business Network and the required suppliers are notified. Suppliers submit their order confirmation through SAP Business Network. The supplier's order confirmation is sent from SAP Business Network to either hub or connected system. As soon as the supplier starts the release and approval process for a purchase order, the status is updated in the connected system. For more information, see: 'Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement' and 'Setting Up Central Procurement with SAP Business Network for Procurement' and SAP Knowledge Base Article here.

back to top

Invoice Management

Alternative Reconciliation Account

Accounts payable accountants can now enter predefined values into the G/L account field on the details screen of the Create Supplier Invoice – Advanced app. The same applies to the reconciliation account field under general information in the Manage Supplier Invoices app. In the Import Supplier Invoices app, the downloaded template now includes the field reconciliation account. Upfront, the configuration expert needs to define the alternative reconciliation accounts allowed for each reconciliation account via SSCUI 'Define Alternative Reconciliation Accounts' (SSCUI ID 102631). For more information, see: Alternative Reconciliation

 

My Inbox: CDS Based Custom Attributes for Supplier Invoices

Your manager needs more information during his daily workflow tasks: As a configuration expert, you can now include custom attributes from the CDS entity I_SupplierInvoiceAPI01 in the expert view in the My Inbox app. Check out the configuration interface 'Maintain CDS Based Custom Attributes' (SSCUI ID 106664). For more information, see: My Inbox and My Inbox Expert View 

back to top

SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Use of Document Information Extraction templates

Document Information Extraction service integrated with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management is an OCR based solution (ML) that enables to automatically extract data from an incoming supplier invoice (PDF or image). The use of Document Information Extraction templates allows users to extract information from invoices, based on the specific layout used by each supplier. These templates help the machine learning model to better identify and extract data fields from invoices that were initially missed or incorrectly processed. This leads to more accurate and efficient data extraction.

When uploading an invoice in the inbound flow of Central Invoice Management, a field on the invoice is not correctly detected. The power user then fixes the extraction results in the document and assigns it to a template. This trains the machine learning model to recognize the correct layout. Once a new invoice - with the same complex layout - is uploaded, the information will be extracted with higher confidence. Now, without any manual adjustments, the invoice can be posted in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This significantly streamlines the process and reduces manual intervention, making invoice management more efficient and reliable. 

Document Information Extraction Templates app is available without any activation required. However, users must be granted with some new authorizations to be able to see and use it. For more information, see:Use Document Information Extraction Templates

Supporting different start and step conditions (amount, PO-based, credit memo)

We increase flexibility by introducing more conditions to initiate the approval workflow because different invoices require different customer-specific approval. We enable customers to define the best approval process for each situation by enabling the common information model (CIM) to deliver several approval criteria that could be used as part of start and step conditions: 

  • Invoice is based on Purchase Order
  • Invoice contains at least one non-purchase order item
  • Supplier invoice type is credit memo
  • Gross Amount in company code currency

For more information, see New Start and Step Conditions for Approval Workflow

Automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts

You can now activate automatic reprocessing of invoices with missing receipts within the 'Manage Processing Services' app. When you enable this process, you can determine the number of reprocessing cycles for invoices with missing receipts. This feature allows for better visibility of invoice statuses and reduces the number of invoices which require manual invoice processing. During the Reference Object Matching process step, the system checks for any missing receipts, either goods receipts or service entry sheets. If no receipt is posted for a purchase order item marked for goods receipt-based invoice verification, the receipt is recognized as missing. The system then moves to the new process step, awaiting receipts. For more information, see : Automatic Reprocessing of Invoices with Missing Receipts

Looking for more innovations on SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management? Check this out: 2502 

back to top

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn 🙂

More innovation can be found in the What's new viewer

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

🚀The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

😎Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!   

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.

🔎 Find All of Our Assets   

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

back to top

entry.title: SAP Finance and Spend Management - Cloud Onboarding Services
entry.content:
